entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: Legume crops used for food and nutritional security

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITIONAL SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: GENETIC GAINS
entities.description: The increase in the average performance of a crop population over time due to selection

entities.name: LEGUME BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Organized efforts to improve legume crops through breeding

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit of input

entities.name: GENOMICS TOOLS
entities.description: Tools used to analyze the genome of an organism to develop improved cultivars

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding method using markers to transfer a specific trait into a recipient variety

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED RECURRENT SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method using markers to accumulate favorable alleles for a trait

entities.name: SIMPLE GENETIC TRAITS
entities.description: Traits controlled by a few genes with simple inheritance patterns

entities.name: COMPLEX TRAITS
entities.description: Traits controlled by multiple genes with complex inheritance patterns

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI
entities.description: Regions of DNA associated with a particular quantitative trait

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method that uses genome-wide markers to predict the breeding value of individuals

entities.name: GENOMIC RESOURCES
entities.description: Data and tools related to the genome of a species

entities.name: TRAINING POPULATIONS
entities.description: A set of individuals used to calibrate genomic selection models

entities.name: GS MODELS
entities.description: Mathematical models used in genomic selection to predict phenotypic values

entities.name: MARKER DENSITY
entities.description: The number of markers per unit length of the genome

entities.name: SIZE OF TRAINING POPULATION
entities.description: The number of individuals included in the training set for genomic selection

entities.name: GENOTYPE AND ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The differential response of genotypes to different environments
